highlights: National historic site built in 1820 with traditional Vietnamese đình architecture and intricate decorative elements
Sacred spiritual center honoring local deities and fallen soldiers from Hoàng Sa and Trường Sa
Rare architectural features including paired stone lions and dual-dragon roof decorations

thingsToKnow.crowds: Very crowded during Lunar New Year boat racing festival (4th-5th of lunar January)
thingsToKnow.accessibility: Located on Lý Sơn island; small structure but easily visible from the dike road
thingsToKnow.seasonal: Annual commemoration ceremony held around lunar February. Boat racing festival during early lunar January draws large crowds

visitorTips: Dress respectfully when visiting; consider making a small incense donation to support maintenance and cultural preservation
Visit during boat racing festival (lunar January 4-5) for vibrant local celebration atmosphere
Bring sun protection; the island has intense sun exposure and limited shade

faq.question: What festivals or ceremonies occur here?
faq.answer: Annual tribute ceremony around lunar February. Lunar New Year boat racing festival (4th-5th) occurs on nearby waters with large local crowds

faq.question: What should I know about visiting as a foreigner?
faq.answer: Dress respectfully to show respect to local residents. Small incense donations support facility maintenance and cultural preservation efforts

faq.question: How do I reach this site?
faq.answer: Located on Lý Sơn island's main village. The small đình is visible from the dike road and easily accessible

sentiment.positive: Visitors praise the well-maintained 300+ year old structure, its cultural and spiritual significance to island residents, helpful caretakers, and authentic local traditions
